campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies have to pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department explained the proposed changes, and the council then discussed and voted on whether to approve the new tax amounts.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Finance department presented the annual tax rate update
Council will vote on the new rates for businesses operating in Campbell
Changes take effect in the next fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public comments, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city rule) and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process, with plans to finalize and adopt it at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
City skipped standard first reading procedure
Final adoption scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the city's finances from February. They also authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services to move forward with town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other service providers
Approved town improvement project planning and preparation

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for May 5th and May 12th, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each meeting, set specific times for certain topics, and checked on any special notice requirements for documents. The council also reviewed public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 5, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned items for the May 12, 2026 draft agenda
Set time-specific slots for certain agenda items to keep meetings on track
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding which neighborhood grant requests would receive funding for the 2026-27 budget year.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comments from residents
Neighborhood meeting attendance updates
Committee reports and staff updates

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special study session on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide public comment both in person at the meeting and online.
keyTopics: Interviews for city board and commission positions
Public comment period for residents (in-person and online)
Study session format to discuss board/commission selections

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from the Economic Development Committee's sessions in January and March. The council also set aside time for residents to speak about any issues they wanted to raise, though the council generally cannot make decisions on topics not formally listed on the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of Economic Development Committee minutes from January 2026
Approval of Economic Development Committee minutes from March 2026
Public comment period for residents on any council matters
State law limitations on decisions for off-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn by staff before a vote could take place.
keyTopics: FIFA World Cup 2026 supergraphic signs around Levi's Stadium
New firefighters union contract for 2026-2029
Staff withdrew one agenda item from consideration

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, to discuss several financial and development matters for the city. They held public hearings on tax rules, the next year's development budget, and user fees, while also reviewing plans to address homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 development budget approval
User fees and service charge updates
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss routine business including staff reports and meeting schedules, and held a public hearing on a proposal to divide a 1-acre vacant lot on Old Trace Road into nine single-family homes plus seven smaller accessory dwelling units. The council also elected new leadership positions for the chair and vice chair.
keyTopics: Subdivision of Old Trace Road property to create 9 new single-family homes
Addition of 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller secondary homes) on the same lot
Election of new City Council chair and vice chair
Director's report and meeting schedule updates
